Off Calendar Sep-22-2020 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-01-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Charles Lin, Esq., counsel for Kan Jianattaso, Petitioner (appearing by video conference).

In response to the Court's concern as to a lack of response to the Examiner's notes, Mr. Lin advises the Court that he did file a response on August 20, 2020 but acknowledges that no courtesy copies of the same were delivered to the Examiner. Mr. Lin confirms that all of the pleadings requested in the Examiner Notes have now been filed. At the Court's query, Mr. Lin confirms that the proposed Administrator is not a California resident but is a U.S. resident and that she is bondable. The Court notes that it will require a full bond. Mr. Lin also confirms that declinations to serve of the decedent's parents have been filed as they are residents of Saudi Arabia.

After further discussion, the Court takes the matter under submission, advising that it will grant the Petition, requiring a full bond, and setting the status for filing of the bond for September 22, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. [no appearance required if the bond is filed] and will set status of closing the estate for February 21, 2022 at 9:00 a.m.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

[Post Script: The Court set status of closing the estate for February 21, 2022. Unbeknowst to the Clerk and consequently, the Court, Feb. 21, 2022 is President's Day - a holiday. consequently, the Clerk has re-set the status hearing for Feb. 22, 2022.
